author: Gurdas Puri, Kushta
title: Qissa-i-Photo, Amritsar Hissa-i-Awwal
gloss: A short Punjabi poem regarding certain houses of ill-fame in brothels, Amritsar.
notes: Urban exposé verse ('Photo of Amritsar') on the brothel quarter; author uses takhallus Kushta. Catalog prints '1sa edition'.

author: Gurdit Singh, Bhai
title: Qissa-i-Sundar Singh Dharwi
gloss: A brief account of the adventures of Sundar Singh, a notorious dacoit of Jagdev, district Amristar.
publisher: B. Chatar Singh Jiwan Singh, Book-sellers
notes: Second dacoit ballad this quarter (compare Jiwana Mor, p10 s256).

author: Hasan Shah, Sayad
title: Hinduon ka Asli Khaka
gloss: Verses deploring the indifference of the Muhammadans towards trade and describing the benefit the Hindus are reaping by it.
notes: Economic-communal polemic ('The Real Portrait of the Hindus') — commerce as communal competition; key homogenization-dynamics data point.

title: Jangnama-i-Guru Gobind Singh Sahib
gloss: The story in verse how Guru Gobind Singh, the 10th Sikh Guru, fought against Aurangzeb, the Mogal Emperor of Delhi.
publisher: B. Ram Nath, Anand Prakash Press
notes: Persian-script counterpart of the Gurmukhi jangnama at p11 s265 — same story, both scripts, same quarter.

title: Jhok Sat Guru Wali
gloss: Verses in praise of Nanak Dev and other Sikh Gurus.
publisher: B. Chatar Singh Jiwan Singh, Book-sellers

author: Jiwan Shah, Mian
title: Jhok Imam-i-Hussain
gloss: Verses describing the martyrdom of Husain, grandson of Muhammad.
notes: Jhok genre used for both Sikh Gurus (s289) and Imam Husain (this) — shared devotional form across communities.

author: Karam Ilahi, Maulvi
title: Fa'awa-i-Maulvi Karam Ilahi, Ma'ruf ba Anwa'-i-Dil Pazir
gloss: A book on Muhammadan precepts on prayer, fasting, alms, marriage, &c.
publisher: Hafiz Muhammad Din and Sons, Book-sellers
copyright: Hafiz Muhammad Din and Sons, Lahore; No. 184, dated the 21st November 1910.
notes: 400-page quarto fatwa collection in Punjabi verse tradition — vernacular fiqh at scale.

author: Muhammad Ibrahim, Maulvi
title: Khutba
gloss: A sermon in Punjabi verse against polytheism with the Khutba read before the 'Id prayers.
publisher: Shaikh Muhammad Din and Hafiz Abdur Rahman
